seoTitle: How to Create a Text Box in Power BI Desktop
metaDescription: Learn how to add text boxes for titles and annotations in Power BI Desktop to give your reports clearer context and structure.
intro: A well-placed text box can turn a chart full of numbers into a report that actually explains itself. This tutorial shows how to create text boxes in Power BI Desktop for titles and annotations, helping your dashboards communicate more clearly.

steps.heading: Insert a text box
steps.body: From the Insert menu in Power BI Desktop, add a text box to your report canvas in the location you want.

steps.heading: Add a title
steps.body: Type a clear, descriptive title into the text box so viewers immediately understand what the report page covers.

steps.heading: Add annotations for context
steps.body: Use additional text boxes near specific visuals to call out important trends, caveats, or explanations.

steps.heading: Apply it to business reporting
steps.body: Use titled, annotated pages for stakeholder-facing reports like sales summaries where extra context helps readers act on the data.

faq.question: Can I format the text in a Power BI text box?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust font, size, color, and alignment within the text box formatting options.

faq.question: Do text boxes update automatically with the data?
faq.answer: No, text boxes are static content, so any references to specific numbers need to be updated manually.

faq.question: Can I link a text box to a bookmark or URL?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI text boxes support adding hyperlinks to external sites or internal bookmarks.

faq.question: Will a text box affect my report's performance?
faq.answer: No, text boxes are lightweight elements and don't add any meaningful load to report performance.
